meta.title: Reported Speech
meta.description: Learn to report what people said using reported speech.
meta.description_ko: 간접화법을 사용하여 다른 사람이 한 말을 전달하는 방법을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: Tenses shift back one step in reported speech.
explanation.ko: 간접화법에서 시제는 한 단계씩 과거로 이동합니다.

questions.prompt: 간접화법에서 동사 시제는 어떻게 변하나요?

explanation.en: 'Am' → 'was' in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'I am tired.' → Reported: 'She said she _____ tired.'

explanation.en: 'Will' → 'would' in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'I will call you.' → Reported speech는?
choices: She said she will call me.
She said she would call me.

explanation.en: Reported statement: subject + said + (that) + past verb.
explanation.ko: 간접 진술: 주어 + said + (that) + 과거 동사.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.

explanation.en: Present perfect → past perfect in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'I have finished.' → Reported: 'She said she _____ the report.'

explanation.en: Yes/no questions: 'if/whether' + subject + verb (no inversion).

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Are you coming?' → Reported는?
choices: She asked if I was coming.
She asked that I was coming.

explanation.en: Reported wh-questions: normal word order + backshift.
explanation.ko: 간접 의문사 질문: 정상 어순 + 시제 이동.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Where do you live?' → 'He asked me where I _____.'

explanation.en: Reported yes/no: 'asked if I wanted' (no auxiliary inversion).
explanation.ko: 간접 예/아니오: 'asked if I wanted'(조동사 도치 없음).

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'She asked me did I want any coffee.'
choices: did I want → if I wanted

explanation.en: Reported negative command: told + object + not to + base verb.
explanation.ko: 간접 부정 명령: told + 목적어 + not to + 동사 원형.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: She told me not to worry.

explanation.en: Pronouns shift based on the speaker's perspective.

questions.prompt: 대명사는 간접화법에서 어떻게 변하나요?

explanation.en: Reported negative command: not to + base verb.
explanation.ko: 간접 부정 명령: not to + 동사 원형.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Don't touch that!' → Reported: 'He told me _____ touch that.'

explanation.en: 'Say' doesn't need an object. 'Tell' needs one: 'He told me...'

questions.prompt: 'say'와 'tell'의 차이(간접화법에서)?
choices: say는 사람 목적어 불필요, tell은 반드시 사람 목적어 필요
say는 반드시 사람 목적어, tell은 불필요

explanation.en: 'Can' → 'could' in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Can you help me?' → 'She asked if I _____ help her.'

explanation.en: Present perfect → past perfect: 'had eaten'.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: He mentioned that he hadn't eaten yet.

explanation.en: now→then, here→there, today→that day, tomorrow→the next day.
explanation.ko: now→then, here→there, today→that day, tomorrow→the next day.

questions.prompt: 'now', 'here', 'today'는 간접화법에서 어떻게 변하나요?

explanation.en: 'Yesterday' → 'the day before' or 'the previous day'.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'I bought this yesterday.' → 'She said she had bought it _____.'

explanation.en: 'Say + person' is wrong. Use 'tell + person'.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'He said me that he was busy.'

explanation.en: 'Going to' → 'was going to' in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'I'm going to quit.' → Reported는?
choices: She said she is going to quit.
She said she was going to quit.
She told she was going to quit.

explanation.en: Present perfect → past perfect: 'had ... been'.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Have you ever been to Paris?' → 'She asked if I _____ ever _____ to Paris.'

explanation.en: 'Warn + object + not to + verb' for warnings.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: She warned me not to drive so fast.

explanation.en: 'Suggest that + subject + should/bare form'.

questions.prompt: 'suggest'를 간접화법에서 쓸 때 올바른 구조는?

explanation.en: After 'suggest that', use base form (or 'should + base form').

questions.prompt: He suggested that we _____ a break. (그는 우리가 휴식을 취하자고 제안했습니다.)

explanation.en: These verbs introduce indirect questions.

questions.prompt: 'ask', 'wonder', 'want to know'는 어떤 간접화법을 이끄나요?

explanation.en: Reported request: 'asked + object + to + base verb'.
explanation.ko: 보고된 요청: 'asked + 목적어 + to + 동사 원형'.

questions.prompt: Direct: 'Please close the door.' → 'He asked me _____ the door.'

explanation.en: Reported statements use 'that' as a conjunction.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: What did the teacher say? B: She said _____ we had to submit by Friday.'
